subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 7: WEB DEVELOPMENT
topics: Web development involves the creation and styling of digital content to ensure it is functional, accessible,
and visually engaging. This topic focuses on Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), the essential design tool that
allows developers to control the presentation of HTML structures.
activities: Computer lab with individual workstations for learners
Space for learners to implement CSS on HTML templates and share screens
Computers with text editor (VS Code, Notepad++) and web browser
Projector and screen to demonstrate CSS transformations
resources: Colour blindness simulation tool (Spectrum or similar extension)
Starter HTML/CSS page designed by learners for accessibility testing
Worksheet to record observations, contrast ratio results, and responsive design issues
Demonstration: Teacher shows how to use DevTools and extensions to test accessibility
Hands-on Practice: Learners test their own pages for colour contrast, readability, and mobile
assessment: Scenario: You are helping a family member build a simple website for their restaurant in Kitwe called
“Mama B’s Kitchen.” You want all page titles (<h1> elements) to be dark green, and the main text

(

) to be dark grey on a light background.
(Applying Style Methods) What is the most professional and efficient way to apply these
styles? Why is this method better than the others, especially if more pages are added later?
